title: Search Unification — merge movie and TV show search into a single 'All Results' section
commit_message: feat: unify movie+TV search into one endpoint — replace separate Movies/TVShows rows with a single 'All Results' section
changed_symbols: movieSearchAPI (callers updated to unifiedSearchAPI — function itself kept for backward compat)
TVShowsSearchAPI (callers updated to unifiedSearchAPI — function itself kept for backward compat)
titles.movies and titles.TVShows (removed — replaced by titles.allResults = 'All Results')

what_the_diff_does.before: Search returns three labelled rows: 'Movies' (movieSearchAPI), 'TV Shows' (TVShowsSearchAPI), and 'Sports' (MatchApi). Two separate HTTP calls are made for movies and TV shows.
what_the_diff_does.after: Search returns two labelled rows: 'All Results' (unifiedSearchAPI with types=0,2, pageSize=15) and 'Sports' (MatchApi unchanged). One HTTP call covers both movies and TV shows.
what_the_diff_does.visible_change: The search results screen drops the 'Movies' and 'TV Shows' row headings and shows all streaming content under a single 'All Results' heading.
what_the_diff_does.compile_note: movieSearchAPI and TVShowsSearchAPI are kept in homescreen.js for backward compatibility with any other callers. Only SearchResults/Component.js stops importing them.

CRITICAL.reason: search-unification.feature.test.js declares all three changed files as sources and contains describe blocks that exactly match the changed symbols: 'unifiedSearchApi' (5 tests), 'unifiedSearchAPI' (5 tests), 'getSeaarchResults — unified search' (8 tests). All 18 are CRITICAL.
tests: unifiedSearchApi > contains types=0,2 to cover both movies and TV shows in one request
unifiedSearchApi > uses the /searchcontent/home path shared with the old separate endpoints
unifiedSearchApi > is distinct from the old Movies endpoint which only had types=0
unifiedSearchApi > is distinct from the old TVShowsSearch endpoint which only had types=2
unifiedSearchApi > ends with & so a query key can be appended directly without extra punctuation
unifiedSearchAPI > calls ApiClient.get exactly once per invocation
unifiedSearchAPI > appends the search key directly after the unifiedSearchApi base URL
unifiedSearchAPI > returns the response object provided by ApiClient.get
unifiedSearchAPI > the URL forwarded to ApiClient.get contains types=0,2
getSeaarchResults — unified search > apiCallling array has exactly 2 entries after the merge
getSeaarchResults — unified search > first entry title is 'All Results'
getSeaarchResults — unified search > first entry searchAPI is unifiedSearchAPI
getSeaarchResults — unified search > first entry apiEndPoint includes pageSize=15
getSeaarchResults — unified search > first entry apiEndPoint includes the search term
getSeaarchResults — unified search > second entry title is 'Sports' and is unchanged
getSeaarchResults — unified search > neither entry has the old 'Movies' title
getSeaarchResults — unified search > neither entry has the old 'TV Shows' title

HIGH.reason: api-navigation.feature.test.js declares src/services/api/common/ApiEndPoints.js as a source — that file is changed (unifiedSearchApi added), so all 30 endpoint contract tests must be re-verified.

SKIP.reason: All remaining test files do not declare ApiEndPoints.js, homescreen.js, or SearchResults/Component.js as a source.

coverage_gaps.gap: Covered by 5 tests in describe('unifiedSearchApi') in search-unification.feature.test.js.
Covered by 5 tests in describe('unifiedSearchAPI') in search-unification.feature.test.js.
Covered by 8 tests in describe('getSeaarchResults — unified search') in search-unification.feature.test.js.

jest_run_note: search-unification.feature.test.js runs 18 CRITICAL tests. api-navigation.feature.test.js runs 30 HIGH tests confirming existing endpoint contracts are intact. All 3 gaps closed.
